name: Archival Keyword Toolkit
summary: A focused toolkit for archival keyword searching across email containers, message files, and general documents. The repo now centers on the current MailHog and FileHog apps, their shared backends, and a smaller current-state documentation set.

tools.name: MailHog
tools.description: Desktop email keyword hunter for PST, OST, EML, MSG, MBOX, and MBX sources with review-friendly export artifacts.

tools.name: FileHog
tools.description: Desktop non-email keyword hunter with filename searching plus content extraction for direct text, OpenXML, PDF, and some office formats.

tools.name: CLI / TUI
tools.description: Terminal entrypoint for the email scanner backend, useful for batch work and smoke checks.

architecture: mailhog/ and filehog/ contain the Wails desktop shells and frontend code.
scanner/ holds the email scanning backend and core MailHog logic.
filescanner/ holds the non-email scanning backend and FileHog logic.
cmd/keyword-hunter-cli/ and tui/ provide the email-side terminal workflow.
scripts/install-commands.sh, scripts/release/linux/, and packaging/linux/ stay aligned with shipping and install behavior.
docs: README.md is the main current-state guide.
TODO.md holds active follow-up work instead of spreading plans across stale notes.
testing/README.md explains the test layout and fixture conventions.
project-dashboard/ provides a lightweight static status view.
dependencies: Go and Node.js are required to build the desktop apps locally.
Linux desktop builds require GTK3 and WebKit2GTK development packages.
MailHog optionally uses readpst and Python 3 plus extract-msg for richer email format support.
FileHog optionally uses pdftotext and LibreOffice for broader content extraction.
openIssues: Automated coverage for PST, OST, MSG, PDF, and LibreOffice-assisted extraction still needs to grow.
Linux desktop launcher behavior needs validation across more desktop environments.
Release validation is still mostly smoke-check driven rather than fully automated.
limitations: Windows support remains deferred and is not treated as a current release target.
Some file-type capabilities degrade to filename-only matching when optional helpers are not installed.
UI-level desktop testing is still light compared with backend package tests.
testing: Package-level Go tests stay near scanner/ and filescanner/.
testing/mailhog/ and testing/filehog/ now provide discoverable generators and runner scripts.
Generated fixtures, outputs, and machine-local sample sets are intentionally ignored.
recentChanges: Removed stale planning docs, handoff notes, and legacy merged-repo leftovers from the tracked repo surface.
Consolidated project guidance into a stronger README plus TODO and dashboard.
Added a tool-oriented testing layout and missing FileHog backend tests.
Tightened .gitignore for generated build and testing artifacts.
